# TNEG

<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_golden_manual.py; do not edit. -->


## Introduction

Elementwise negation of a tile.

## Math Interpretation

For each element `(i, j)` in the valid region:

$$ \mathrm{dst}_{i,j} = -\mathrm{src}_{i,j} $$

## Assembly Syntax

PTO-AS form: see [PTO-AS Specification](assembly.md).

Synchronous form:

```text
%dst = tneg %src : !pto.tile<...>
```

### AS Level 1 (SSA)

```text
%dst = pto.tneg %src : !pto.tile<...> -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### AS Level 2 (DPS)

```text
pto.tneg ins(%src : !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%dst : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```

### IR Level 1 (SSA)

```text
%dst = pto.tneg %src : !pto.tile<...> -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### IR Level 2 (DPS)

```text
pto.tneg ins(%src : !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%dst : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```
## C++ Intrinsic

C++ declaration:

```cpp
template <typename TileDataDst, typename TileDataSrc>
PTO_INST void TNEG(TileDataDst &dst, TileDataSrc &src);
```

## Constraints

- The op iterates over `dst.GetValidRow()` / `dst.GetValidCol()`.

## Examples

```cpp
#include <pto/pto-inst.hpp>

using namespace pto;
void example() {
  using TileT = Tile<TileType::Vec, float, 16, 16>;
  TileT x, out;
  TNEG(out, x);
}
```

## ASM Form Examples

### Auto Mode

```text
# Auto mode: compiler/runtime-managed placement and scheduling.
%dst = pto.tneg %src : !pto.tile<...> -> !pto.tile<...>
```


### PTO Assembly Form

```text
%dst = tneg %src : !pto.tile<...>
# AS Level 2 (DPS)
pto.tneg ins(%src : !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%dst : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```
